name: Valle del Lozoya MTB Center - Route 3
name_original: Embalse de Pinilla – circular desde Lozoya
description: Hike the moderate 9.9-mile Valle del Lozoya MTB Center - Route 3, gaining 327 feet with reservoir views and diverse forests.
region_intro: Hiking around Lozoya offers access to diverse natural landscapes within the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park. The region is characterized by mountainous terrain, including peaks over 2,000 meters, extensive forests of Scots pine, oak, and juniper, and the central Lozoya River. This valley features varied ecosystems, from high Mediterranean mountain environments to riverine areas, providing a range of hiking experiences. Best hiking trails around Lozoya The most popular hiking route is Valle del Lozoya MTB Center - Route 3, a 9.9 miles (15.9 km) trail that takes 4 hours 8 minutes to complete, offering moderate terrain through the valley. Another top favourite among local hikers is Pico del Nevero y Alto de Porrinoso — circular desde el Puerto de Navafría: Parque Natural Sierra Norte de Guadarrama, a moderate 7.2 miles (11.6 km) path. This route provides views of the Sierra Norte de Guadarrama. Local hikers also love the Pico del Nevero and Alto del Porrinoso Loop, a 6.8 miles (11.0 km) trail leading through mountainous landscapes, often completed in about 4 hours 41 minutes. Hiking around Lozoya is defined by mountainous peaks, dense pine forests, and the Lozoya River valley. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate valley paths to more difficult mountain ascents. The routes in Lozoya are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 150 reviews. More than 1000 hikers have used komoot to explore Lozoya's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Lozoya?
A: Lozoya offers a wide variety of hiking experiences, with over 35 trails documented on komoot. These range from easy strolls to challenging mountain ascents, catering to different fitness levels.

Q: Are there challenging hikes for experienced trekkers in Lozoya?
A: Yes, Lozoya provides several challenging routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e957312087/cerro-de-la-cruz-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro de la Cruz Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 30.7 km trail with significant elevation changes, offering a demanding experience. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1153781642/mirador-cerro-las-coronas-y-presa-ceguilla-circular-por-el-parque-nacional-sierra-de-guadarrama" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador Cerro Las Coronas y Presa Ceguilla — circular por el Parque Nacional Sierra de Guadarrama&lt;/a&gt;, a 21.6 km difficult route.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Lozoya?
A: Absolutely. Many trails in Lozoya are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1154708610/pico-del-nevero-y-alto-de-porrinoso-circular-desde-el-puerto-de-navafria-parque-natural-sierra-norte-de-guadarrama" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico del Nevero y Alto de Porrinoso — circular desde el Puerto de Navafría&lt;/a&gt;, which offers stunning views, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e957845513/pico-del-nevero-and-alto-del-porrinoso-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico del Nevero and Alto del Porrinoso Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any waterfalls to see on hikes in Lozoya?
A: Yes, the Lozoya region is home to several beautiful waterfalls. You can explore highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/851377" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Purgatory Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3028592" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chorrera de San Mamés&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6004998" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascada Mayor of the Sierra de Guadarrama&lt;/a&gt;. These natural features add a scenic element to many routes.

Q: What kind of natural landmarks or viewpoints can I discover while hiking in Lozoya?
A: Lozoya, situated within the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park, offers diverse landscapes. You can reach mountain peaks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4078211" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nevero Peak&lt;/a&gt;, or cross mountain passes such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2347605" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Linera&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4003184" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Navafría Pass&lt;/a&gt;. The region is also known for its extensive forests of Scots pine, oak, and juniper, and the central Lozoya River valley.

Q: When is the best time of year to hike in Lozoya?
A: Lozoya offers hiking opportunities year-round, but the best time depends on your preference. Spring and autumn provide pleasant temperatures and vibrant colors, especially in the beech forests like Hayedo de Montejo. Summer is ideal for higher altitude trails, while winter can offer snowy landscapes for experienced hikers, particularly in the Peñalara massif.

Q: What kind of wildlife can I expect to see while hiking in Lozoya?
A: The Lozoya Valley is rich in biodiversity. Hikers might spot deer, wild boar, and Spanish Ibex. The region is also a haven for birdwatchers, with species like the imperial eagle, golden eagle, and Eurasian black vulture. The Sierra de Guadarrama National Park protects nearly 1,300 animal species.

Q: Are there family-friendly or easy hikes suitable for children in Lozoya?
A: Yes, Lozoya has trails suitable for families and children. The Lozoya Valley Nature Trail offers a main route and additional itineraries that are generally accessible. Areas like Hoya Encavera along the Lozoya River provide easy walks with shady meadows and river access, perfect for a relaxed outing.

Q: Are hiking trails in Lozoya dog-friendly?
A: Many trails in Lozoya are suitable for dogs, especially the less strenuous routes through the valley and forests. However, it's always recommended to check specific trail regulations within the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park, as some protected areas might have restrictions or require dogs to be on a leash to protect wildlife.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Lozoya?
A: The komoot community highly rates hiking in Lozoya, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 150 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse natural landscapes, from mountainous peaks to dense pine forests and the scenic Lozoya River valley. The variety of routes catering to different ability levels is also frequently highlighted.

Q: Is public transport available to access hiking trails in Lozoya?
A: While specific public transport details for every trailhead vary, the Lozoya region does have bus services connecting to some towns and villages within the valley. From these points, many trails are accessible. It's advisable to check local bus schedules for the most up-to-date information on routes and timings to specific starting points.

Q: Are there cultural or historical sites along the hiking trails in Lozoya?
A: Yes, the region integrates historical elements into its natural beauty. Hikers can encounter medieval bridges, such as the historic Puente Congosto, and visit the Monastery of Santa Maria de El Paular, which is a significant cultural landmark. An arboretum near the monastery is also worth exploring, especially in spring and fall.
tips: Site on the side of the road that offers us one of the best panoramic views of the Pinilla reservoir and the town of Lozoya. Following the same path, you can go around the reservoir completely, a very quiet walk without difficulty that, depending on the time of year, offers unique and very picturesque landscapes.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on Valle del Lozoya MTB Center - Route 3?
faqs.answer: The route generally features a mix of tracks (pistas), paths (caminos), and some singletrack (senderos). While primarily designed for mountain biking, for hikers, this translates to varied footing. Some sections may have stony areas, but overall, the trail is considered to have little technical difficulty for hiking.

faqs.question: Are there any notable viewpoints or landmarks along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route offers picturesque scenery. You'll enjoy views of the Lozoya River and the Pinilla reservoir. A specific highlight is the Pinilla Reservoir Viewpoint. The valley also features centennial pine forests, Pyrenean oak groves, and offers mountain vistas.

faqs.question: How long does it take to hike this route?
faqs.answer: This route is approximately 15.9 kilometers (9.9 miles) long. For hiking, it typically takes around 4 hours and 8 minutes to complete, depending on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: Is Valle del Lozoya MTB Center - Route 3 dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While specific rules for this exact route are not detailed, trails in the Lozoya Valley are generally accessible for dogs. It's always best to keep your dog on a leash, especially when sharing the trail with mountain bikers and to protect the local wildlife.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The Lozoya Valley is beautiful year-round. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant colors. Summer can be warm, so an early start is advisable. Winter hiking is possible, but be prepared for colder conditions and potential snow, especially at higher elevations.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: Specific parking details for this exact route are not provided, but the Valle del Lozoya MTB Center routes typically start from accessible points within the Lozoya Valley. Look for designated parking areas near the villages or main access points to the valley, such as around Lozoya or Pinilla del Valle.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike the trails in the Lozoya Valley, including this route. However, it's always good practice to check for any local regulations or temporary restrictions before your visit.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route is part of the "Centro BTT "Valle del Lozoya" - Ruta 3" and also shares significant sections with the "Camino Natural del Valle del Lozoya" and "Pueblos del Valle de Lozoya" trails. You might also briefly encounter parts of "Centro BTT "Valle del Lozoya" - Ruta 2" and "Entorno al Cerro de la Cruz".

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: The route is graded as 'moderate' for hiking. While it doesn't have significant elevation gain (around 100 meters), its length of nearly 16 kilometers might be challenging for very young children or absolute beginners. Hikers should have good physical fitness and be prepared for a varied path.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife or plants can I expect to see?
faqs.answer: The Lozoya Valley is rich in biodiversity. You might spot various bird species, including the endangered imperial eagle. The area is also home to unique insects like the Graellsia moth. The landscape features diverse flora, including centennial pine forests, Pyrenean oak groves, poplars, and ash trees.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities like cafes or restaurants nearby?
faqs.answer: The Lozoya Valley has several charming villages, such as Lozoya and Pinilla del Valle, which offer cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options. These are typically found near the main access points to the valley, making it convenient to find refreshments before or after your hike.
